hints.genitive: Genitive singular of 'spiritus' is also 'spiritus' (-us), but here it is the subject
hints.accusative: Accusative singular would be 'spiritum' — 'Spiritus' is nominative here
hints.ablative: Ablative singular would be 'spiritu' — 'Spiritus' is nominative here

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'spiritus' has genitive in -us
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns ending in -us have genitive in -i — 'spiritus' gen. is 'spiritus'
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'spiritus' has genitive in -us

hints.nominative: Nominative of 'Dominus' is 'Dominus' — 'Domini' is the genitive singular
hints.dative: Dative of 'Dominus' is 'Domino' — 'Domini' is genitive singular
hints.vocative: Vocative of 'Dominus' is 'Domine' — 'Domini' is genitive singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'Domini' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'Domini' ends in -i

hints.nominative: Nominative of the 1st person pronoun is 'ego' — 'me' is accusative/ablative
hints.ablative: 'me' can be ablative, but after 'super' meaning 'upon' it takes the accusative
hints.dative: Dative of the 1st person pronoun is 'mihi' — 'me' here is accusative

steps.prompt: This phrase has no finite verb. What verb is implied?

hints.erat (was): The implied verb is present tense 'est' — the Spirit IS upon me
hints.venit (comes): While the Spirit does come, the implied copula here is 'est'
hints.habet (has): 'habet' would need a different construction — the implied verb is 'est'

translation: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me
